zh.lifeSummary: William Jesse於1619年出生在英國素瑞黑。他於1644年6月12日與Susanna在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州溫莎女爵結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1645年10月29日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德素費爾德享年26歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州哈普登斯普林菲爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Jesse于1619年出生在英国素瑞黑。他于1644年6月12日与Susanna在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州溫莎女爵结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1645年10月29日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德素費爾德享年26岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州哈普登斯普林菲爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Jesse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German: from a short form of the personal name Jesper, a Low German form of Kaspar , an equivalent of Casper .
South German: from a shortened form of the personal name Johannes (see John ).

variants.name: Jessee
meanings: English: variant of   Jesse  , pronounced as two syllables. This surname is most common in VA.

variants.name: Jessel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Heinz.

English: perhaps a variant of Chessell with voicing of initial /ʧ/ to /ʤ/, spelled J-. This is a habitational name, either from Chesthill, a lost place in Moreton Say and Stoke upon Tern (Shropshire) or from Chessell, Freshwater (Isle of Wight). Both placenames are spelled as Chesthull in medieval records but the Shropshire name is thought to refer to a hill (Old English hyll) where parish boundaries were disputed (the first element is Old English ceast ‘strife, contention’), whereas the Isle of Wight name refers to a hill where burial chests or coffins were found (the first element is Old English cest ‘chest’), probably referring to the 6th-century pagan cemetery on Chessell Down.

variants.name: Kaspar
meanings: German; Czech and Slovak (Kašpar); Slovenian and Sorbian (also Kašpar): from the personal names Kaspar (German) and Kašpar (Czech and Sorbian; also a variant of Slovak Gašpar and Slovenian Gašper), equivalents of Casper . See also Kasper , Gaspar , and Gasper .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: William Jesse was born in 1619, in Shere, Surrey, England. He married Susanna on 12 June 1644, in Windsor, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 29 October 1645, in Suffield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 26, and was buried in Springfield, Hampden, Massachusetts, United States.

Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 29 octobre 1645, à Suffield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 26 ans, et a été enterré à Springfield, Hampden, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

ja.lifeSummary: William Jesseは1619年にイギリススリーシェアで生まれた。1644年6月12日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ウィンドソルでSusannaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1645年10月29日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードスフィールドで26歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ハンプデンスプリングフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Jesse(은)는 1619년 영국 수리 셔에서 태어났다. 그는 1644년 6월 12일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 윈드소르에서 Susanna(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1645년 10월 29일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 수필드에서 26세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 함p든 스프링필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Jesse nasceu em 1619, em Shere, Surrey, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Susanna em 12 de junho de 1644, em Windsor, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 29 de outubro de 1645, em Suffield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 26 anos, e foi sepultado em Springfield, Hampden, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: William Jesse родился в 1619 г. в местности шир, сурри, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Susanna 12 июня 1644 г. в местности виндзор, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 29 октября 1645 г. в местности суффиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 26 лет и был похоронен в местности спрингфиелд, хампден, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.
